title: NTC Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The dryer's temperature sensor is providing out-of-range readings to the control board.
fullDescription: Error E01 indicates the NTC temperature sensor in the dryer drum is sending readings that are outside the expected range. The control board cannot properly regulate drying temperature, which may result in overheating or insufficient drying. The sensor, its wiring, or the connector may be faulty.

causes.cause: NTC temperature sensor has failed
Sensor wiring is damaged or loose
Sensor connector is corroded
Control board sensor input circuit is faulty

fixSteps.title: Reset the dryer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer for 5 minutes. Reconnect and run a test cycle. If E01 returns, proceed with diagnosis.

fixSteps.title: Locate the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: The NTC sensor is typically mounted inside the drum housing or near the exhaust duct. Access by removing the top or rear panel depending on the model.

fixSteps.title: Test the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor and measure resistance with a multimeter. At room temperature (25C/77F), it should read approximately 10-50k ohms. OL or zero readings indicate failure.

fixSteps.title: Replace the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the sensor tests out of range, replace it. Remove the mounting clip or screw, disconnect wiring, and install the new sensor.

whenToCallPro: If replacing the sensor does not resolve E01, the control board may need professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch dryer E01 NTC temperature sensor error fix

faq.q: Can E01 cause the dryer to overheat?
faq.a: Yes. If the sensor reads too low, the control board may keep the heater on longer than needed. If you notice excessive heat, stop using the dryer until repaired.

faq.q: Is the NTC sensor the same as a thermostat?
faq.a: No. The NTC sensor provides a continuous temperature reading, while a thermostat is a switch that opens or closes at specific temperatures. They serve complementary functions.

faq.q: How much does an NTC sensor cost?
faq.a: The sensor itself is inexpensive, typically $10-$30 for the part.

title: Heating Element Failure
shortDescription: The dryer's heating element has failed or its circuit is broken.
fullDescription: Error E04 indicates the dryer's heating element is not producing heat. The control board detects that the temperature is not rising after activating the heater. This could be a burned-out element, a tripped thermal fuse, or a failed relay on the control board.

causes.cause: Heating element has burned out or broken
Thermal fuse has tripped
High-limit thermostat has tripped
Control board heater relay has failed

symptoms: Dryer runs but produces no heat
Clothes remain damp after full cycle
Drum turns normally but air is cold

fixSteps.title: Check thermal fuse
fixSteps.instruction: Access the thermal fuse (usually on the blower housing or exhaust duct). Test continuity with a multimeter. If open (no continuity), replace the fuse. Note: a blown thermal fuse often indicates a blocked vent.

fixSteps.title: Check high-limit thermostat
fixSteps.instruction: The high-limit thermostat is near the heating element. Test for continuity. If open, replace it. Also check the operating thermostat for proper function.

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the element wires and measure resistance. Normal readings are 10-50 ohms depending on the model. OL indicates a broken element. Also check for ground faults.

fixSteps.title: Clean the exhaust vent
fixSteps.instruction: A blocked vent causes overheating, which trips safety devices. Clean the entire vent path from the dryer to the outside. Check the lint trap, exhaust hose, and exterior vent hood.

whenToCallPro: If all heating components test fine, the control board relay may be faulty, requiring professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch dryer E04 no heat heating element fix

faq.q: Why does the thermal fuse keep blowing?
faq.a: A repeatedly tripping thermal fuse almost always indicates a blocked or restricted exhaust vent. Clean the entire vent system thoroughly.

faq.q: Can I bypass the thermal fuse?
faq.a: Never bypass the thermal fuse. It is a safety device that prevents dryer fires. Replace it and fix the root cause.

faq.q: How long does a dryer heating element last?
faq.a: Typically 8-12 years with normal use. Restricted venting can shorten its life significantly.

title: Exhaust Temperature Too High
shortDescription: The exhaust air temperature has exceeded the safe maximum limit.
fullDescription: Error E05 is a safety alert indicating the exhaust air temperature in the dryer has exceeded the maximum safe threshold. This typically means the airflow through the dryer is severely restricted, causing heat to build up. The most common cause is a blocked lint trap or clogged exhaust vent.

causes.cause: Exhaust vent is clogged with lint
Lint trap/filter is full or damaged
Exhaust hose is kinked or crushed
Blower wheel is broken or obstructed

symptoms: Dryer extremely hot to the touch
Clothes excessively hot when cycle stops

fixSteps.title: Clean the lint trap
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and clean the lint trap thoroughly. If the mesh is clogged with fabric softener residue, wash it with warm soapy water and a soft brush.

fixSteps.title: Clean the exhaust vent
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the exhaust hose from the dryer and the wall. Use a vent cleaning brush kit to clean the entire vent run from the dryer to the exterior. Clear the exterior vent hood of any obstructions.

fixSteps.title: Check the exhaust hose
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the flexible exhaust hose for kinks, crushing, or excessive length. Replace vinyl/foil hoses with rigid or semi-rigid metal duct for better airflow.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the blower wheel
fixSteps.instruction: If the vent is clear but airflow is still poor, check the blower wheel inside the dryer. A broken or lint-clogged blower will not move enough air.

whenToCallPro: If the vent system is long or has multiple turns, a professional vent cleaning service can ensure complete clearing. If the blower wheel needs replacement, a tech can handle it efficiently.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch dryer E05 overheating exhaust vent cleaning fix

faq.q: Is E05 a fire hazard?
faq.a: Yes. An overheating dryer is a significant fire risk. Stop using the dryer immediately, clean the exhaust system, and resolve the issue before running it again.

faq.q: How often should I clean the dryer vent?
faq.a: Clean the lint trap after every load and the full exhaust vent at least once a year. More frequent cleaning is needed if you dry a lot of loads.

faq.q: Can I use a vinyl exhaust hose?
faq.a: Vinyl and foil hoses are not recommended. They can sag, collect lint, and are fire hazards. Use rigid or semi-rigid metal duct for the exhaust.

title: Motor Error
shortDescription: The dryer's drum motor is not operating correctly or has failed.
fullDescription: Error E08 indicates the control board cannot properly run the drum motor. The motor may not start, may stall during operation, or may draw excessive current. This prevents the drum from turning and the dryer from functioning.

causes.cause: Motor capacitor has failed
Motor windings are burned out
Motor is overheating and triggering thermal protection
Drum belt is too tight or drum is jammed

symptoms: Motor hums but drum does not move
Dryer starts then stops within seconds

fixSteps.title: Allow motor to cool
fixSteps.instruction: If the motor has been running, allow it to cool for 30 minutes. Overheated motors trigger thermal protection. A clogged lint system can cause motor overheating.

fixSteps.title: Check drum rotation
fixSteps.instruction: Open the door and try rotating the drum by hand. It should turn smoothly. If it is stuck or very stiff, check for foreign objects or a jammed drum support.

fixSteps.title: Test the motor capacitor
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the motor start/run capacitor. Test with a multimeter on capacitance mode. The reading should match the rating on the capacitor label. Replace if the reading is more than 10% off.

fixSteps.title: Test the motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect motor wiring and test each winding for resistance (typically 2-8 ohms). Also check for ground faults between any winding and the motor housing.

whenToCallPro: If the motor has burned out, replacement involves significant disassembly and is best handled by a professional.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch dryer E08 motor error drum not spinning fix

faq.q: Can a motor capacitor cause E08?
faq.a: Yes. A failed capacitor is one of the most common causes. The motor cannot start without it. Capacitor replacement is inexpensive and straightforward.

faq.q: Why does the motor overheat?
faq.a: Usually due to restricted airflow from a clogged lint system. The motor works harder to move air through blocked vents, causing overheating.

faq.q: How long does a dryer motor last?
faq.a: Typically 10-15 years with proper vent maintenance. Poor airflow significantly shortens motor life.

title: Drum Belt Error
shortDescription: The drum belt switch has detected that the belt is broken or missing.
fullDescription: Error E09 indicates the drum belt switch (also called the belt tensioner switch) has detected that the belt is no longer intact or properly tensioned. Without the belt, the drum cannot rotate even though the motor runs. The dryer has a safety switch that prevents operation without a functional belt.

causes.cause: Drive belt has broken
Belt tensioner spring has broken
Belt switch is faulty
Idler pulley has seized, causing belt to slip off

symptoms: Motor may briefly hum then stop
Burning rubber smell if belt was slipping

fixSteps.title: Verify the belt
fixSteps.instruction: Open the dryer door and try turning the drum by hand. If it spins very freely with no resistance, the belt is likely broken.

fixSteps.title: Access the belt
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the top panel and front panel of the dryer to expose the drum and belt path. Inspect the belt for breakage or severe wear.

fixSteps.title: Check idler pulley and tensioner
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the idler pulley for smooth rotation and the tensioner spring for proper tension. A seized pulley causes excessive belt wear.

fixSteps.title: Replace the belt
fixSteps.instruction: Route the new belt around the drum, under the idler pulley, and around the motor pulley. Ensure correct belt rib orientation. Reassemble panels.

whenToCallPro: Belt replacement is a common DIY repair. Call a pro if you are uncomfortable disassembling the dryer panels or routing the belt.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch dryer E09 belt broken replacement fix

faq.q: How do I know if the belt is broken?
faq.a: If the drum spins very freely by hand with no resistance (normally there is slight resistance from the belt), the belt is broken.

faq.q: How long does a dryer belt last?
faq.a: Typically 5-12 years depending on usage. You may notice squeaking or thumping sounds before it breaks.

faq.q: Should I replace the idler pulley when replacing the belt?
faq.a: It is good practice to replace the idler pulley at the same time, as a worn pulley can quickly damage a new belt.

title: Condensation System Error (Condenser Dryers)
shortDescription: The condensation water system is not functioning properly in condenser dryer models.
fullDescription: Error E18 applies to Bosch condenser dryers (ventless models). It indicates the condensation collection and drainage system is not working correctly. This could mean the water tank is full and not draining, the condensation pump has failed, or the drain hose is blocked.

causes.cause: Condensation tank is full and not emptied
Condensation pump has failed
Drain hose is kinked or blocked
Condenser unit is clogged with lint

symptoms: Dryer runs but does not dry effectively

fixSteps.title: Empty the condensation tank
fixSteps.instruction: Pull out the condensation water tank (usually at the top-left of the dryer). Empty the water, clean the tank, and reinsert it securely.

fixSteps.title: Clean the condenser unit
fixSteps.instruction: Access the condenser unit (usually behind a lower panel). Remove it and rinse under running water to clear lint and dust. Let it dry before reinstalling.

fixSteps.title: Check the drain hose (if plumbed)
fixSteps.instruction: If the dryer is plumbed to a drain, check that the drain hose is not kinked or blocked. Flush the hose with water to clear any obstructions.

fixSteps.title: Test the condensation pump
fixSteps.instruction: Listen for the pump running during a cycle. If it does not activate, test it for electrical continuity and proper voltage supply.

whenToCallPro: If the pump has failed, replacement requires some disassembly. A professional can test and replace it efficiently.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch condenser dryer E18 water tank pump error fix

faq.q: How often should I empty the condensation tank?
faq.a: Empty it after every cycle or at minimum every 2-3 loads. A full tank triggers E18 and stops the dryer.

faq.q: Can I connect the condensation drain to a pipe?
faq.a: Yes. Most Bosch condenser dryers have an option to plumb the drain directly to a standpipe, eliminating the need to manually empty the tank.

faq.q: How often should I clean the condenser?
faq.a: Clean the condenser unit at least once a month. A lint-clogged condenser reduces efficiency and can trigger E18.

title: Communication Error Between Control Boards
shortDescription: The main control board and display/interface board cannot communicate.
fullDescription: Error E32 indicates a communication failure between the main control board and the user interface (display) board. These boards communicate over a data bus, and E32 means the link is broken or experiencing errors. This could be caused by a loose ribbon cable, damaged wiring, or a failed board.

causes.cause: Ribbon cable or connector is loose
Ribbon cable is damaged
User interface board has failed
Main control board has failed

symptoms: Display is blank or shows garbled text

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer for 15 minutes to allow a full reset. Reconnect and see if E32 clears.

fixSteps.title: Check cable connections
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the top panel. Locate the ribbon cable connecting the interface board to the main board. Reseat both connectors firmly.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for damage
fixSteps.instruction: Check the ribbon cable for visible damage such as cuts, kinks, or corrosion on the connector pins. Replace if damaged.

fixSteps.title: Replace cable or board
fixSteps.instruction: If the cable is good, one of the boards may have failed. This typically requires professional diagnosis to determine which board is at fault.

whenToCallPro: If reseating or replacing the cable does not fix E32, a professional can diagnose which control board has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch dryer E32 communication error control board fix

faq.q: Can vibration cause E32?
faq.a: Yes. Dryer vibration over time can loosen ribbon cable connectors. Reseating the connectors is the first and simplest fix.

faq.q: Is E32 expensive to fix?
faq.a: If it is just a loose cable, the fix is free. If a board has failed, costs range from $200-$450 including parts and labor.

faq.q: Can I use the dryer with E32?
faq.a: No. E32 prevents the dryer from operating since the main board and display cannot communicate.

title: Control Board Hardware Error
shortDescription: The main control board has detected an internal hardware fault.
fullDescription: Error E64 indicates the main electronic control board has experienced a hardware failure. A component on the board has failed, preventing normal operation. The dryer will not run until the board is repaired or replaced.

causes.cause: Control board component failure (capacitor, relay, IC)
Power surge damaged the board
Moisture damage on the board
Solder joint failure due to thermal stress

symptoms: Dryer will not start or respond to controls
May see burned or discolored area on the board

fixSteps.title: Power reset attempt
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 30 minutes. Though unlikely with E64, a complete reset occasionally clears a transient hardware fault.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the board visually
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the top panel and inspect the control board for visible damage: burned components, swollen capacitors, discolored areas, or moisture.

fixSteps.title: Check for moisture source
fixSteps.instruction: If water damage is visible, identify and fix the moisture source before installing a new board. Check vent connections and condensation routing.

fixSteps.title: Replace the control board
fixSteps.instruction: Order the correct replacement. Disconnect all connectors from the old board (photograph first for reference). Install the new board and reconnect all connectors.

whenToCallPro: Board replacement is recommended as a professional job. Incorrect installation can damage the new board.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch dryer E64 control board hardware error replacement

faq.q: Is it worth replacing the control board?
faq.a: If the dryer is under 8 years old, yes. Board replacement at $200-$500 is significantly less than a new dryer.

faq.q: Can a board repair shop fix E64?
faq.a: Possibly. Some electronics repair services can fix individual components on appliance boards for less than a new board.

faq.q: Can a surge protector prevent E64?
faq.a: A surge protector can help prevent power surge damage. However, not all E64 failures are surge-related.

title: Control Board Software Error
shortDescription: The control board firmware or software has encountered an unrecoverable error.
fullDescription: Error E65 indicates a software or firmware error in the control board that the system cannot automatically recover from. This differs from E64 (hardware) in that the electronic components may be fine but the software state has become corrupted.

causes.cause: Power interruption corrupted firmware memory
Firmware bug triggered by specific conditions
EEPROM on the board has degraded
Electrical noise from other appliances corrupting data

symptoms: Error may appear and disappear intermittently

fixSteps.title: Extended power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer for 30 minutes. This gives the processor time to fully discharge and perform a cold restart which can clear corrupted memory.

fixSteps.title: Factory reset sequence
fixSteps.instruction: Some Bosch dryers support a factory reset through a specific button combination. Consult your model's manual or try holding the Start button for 10 seconds while powering on.

fixSteps.title: Check power quality
fixSteps.instruction: Measure the outlet voltage. Ensure stable 120V (or 240V for 240V models). Voltage fluctuations during operation can corrupt firmware.

fixSteps.title: Board replacement if persistent
fixSteps.instruction: If E65 returns after every reset, the board memory is permanently corrupted and the board needs replacement or professional reprogramming.

whenToCallPro: If a power reset does not clear E65, the board needs professional reprogramming or replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch dryer E65 software error control board reset fix

faq.q: Will a reset fix E65?
faq.a: Often yes. An extended power reset (30 minutes) clears most software errors caused by power glitches. If E65 returns, the board may need replacement.

faq.q: Can I update the firmware myself?
faq.a: No. Bosch dryer firmware updates require professional service equipment. Only authorized technicians can reprogram the control board.

faq.q: Is E65 covered under warranty?
faq.a: If the dryer is within the warranty period, control board issues including E65 should be covered. Contact Bosch customer service.
